key_themes: Aggressive shareholder returns (13% dividend hike and $2B buyback reset)
$774M opioid settlement creating GAAP net loss overhang
Soft FY2026 identical sales guidance (0-1%) amid competition and consumer pressure
Q4 adjusted EPS beat despite revenue miss and pharmacy/IRA headwinds
summary: Albertsons (ACI) posted mixed Q4/FY2025 results with an adjusted EPS beat of $0.48 but a large GAAP net loss from its $774M opioid settlement. Management countered with a 13% dividend increase to $0.17, a reset of share repurchases to $2B, and a $2B multi-year capex plan centered on AI, productivity, loyalty, and digital initiatives. FY2026 guidance was cautious (identical sales 0-1%, adj. EPS $2.22–$2.32), prompting several analyst price-target cuts, yet the stock trades near $16.70 with an attractive ~4% yield and consensus PT above $21, supporting mildly constructive long-term sentiment despite near-term pressures.
sources: 15+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, company releases, TipRanks), ~15 X posts (primarily low-engagement earnings reactions; limited broad investor discussion)
